    Signal ChainSession Template

Gear Profile

Roland VT-3

Roland VT-3 Voice Transformer — compact vocal processor with 9 voice characters, built-in USB audio interface, and MIDI control for vocoder pitch and preset recall.

Official Site Back to Library

Core Controls

  • Pitch slider: large, central slider that shifts vocal pitch up or down in real time — from a full octave down to an octave up.
  • Formant slider: changes the vocal tract character independently of pitch — shift the formant up to sound smaller/higher, down to sound larger/lower. Combine with Pitch for completely transformed voice textures.
  • Reverb slider: built-in reverb depth for the output signal — adds space and dimension without needing a separate effects unit.
  • Mix slider: wet/dry ratio between the processed signal and the direct input. Useful for subtle enhancement (Mix low) or full transformation (Mix at max).

Voice Characters

  • Vocoder (VP-330 style): classic vocoder effect that speech-modulates a carrier. No keyboard required — the VT-3 generates an internal carrier. Connect the Launchkey 37's MIDI Out to the VT-3's USB MIDI input to play vocoder pitch musically in real time.
  • Auto-Pitch (Classic): melodic pitch correction with a natural-sounding feel — subtle tuning assistance with a musical character.
  • Auto-Pitch (Hard): stepped, quantised pitch tuning for the well-known hard pitch correction sound.
  • Scatter: glitchy, fragmented, broken-sounding effect — the voice is scattered across time in short repeating bursts.
  • Megaphone: narrow bandwidth, distorted telephone/bullhorn character.
  • Radio: AM-style radio signal simulation with noise and bandwidth limiting.
  • Robot: monotone robotic pitched voice — classic science fiction vocal character. Pitch is set by the Pitch slider.
  • Synth Lead: transforms the voice into a synthesizer lead tone, tracking vocal pitch.
  • Synth Bass: sub-octave synth bass derived from the vocal input — speak or sing to generate bass lines.

6 User Presets

Store up to 6 complete snapshots (voice character + Pitch + Formant + Reverb + Mix settings) to the VT-3’s internal memory. Recall any preset in one button press during a performance. Presets can also be recalled via MIDI Program Change messages from Ableton or a MIDI footswitch for hands-free switching.

Built-in USB Audio Interface

The VT-3 functions as a secondary audio interface when connected via USB:

  • The processed vocal output appears as a separate USB audio stream — record it directly into Ableton as a clean digital take alongside the analog feed into MOTU inputs 3/4.
  • Loopback recording routes the final processed signal back into the DAW without any additional cables — useful for capturing the VT-3 output in isolation for mixing separately.

MIDI Control (v1.1+)

  • Vocoder pitch via USB MIDI: connect the Launchkey 37 MIDI Out → VT-3 USB MIDI In (or MOTU MIDI Out → VT-3 MIDI In). Notes played on the keyboard set the vocoder's carrier pitch in real time — play a melody and speak into the SM58 for polyphonic-sounding vocoder phrases.
  • Control Change: automate VT-3 Pitch, Formant, Reverb, and Mix parameters from Ableton via MIDI CC for repeatable, automated vocal effects.
  • Program Change: switch between the 6 user presets from an Ableton Clip's MIDI output, a MIDI footswitch, or the Launchkey 37's Custom Mode pads.

Signal Chain

Live/studio routing:

  1. SM58 (XLR) → VT-3 XLR/TRS combo input (phantom power OFF — SM58 is dynamic).
  2. VT-3 monitor output → performer’s headphones or stage monitor for real-time wet/dry feedback without going through the computer.
  3. VT-3 stereo line output → MOTU inputs 3/4 (line level — set MOTU gain to 12 o’clock).
  4. VT-3 USB → computer for separate digital stream if dual-capture is desired.

Dual recording for maximum flexibility:

  • Audio Track A (clean): SM58 → MOTU input 1 → Ableton Audio Track. Dry, unprocessed signal preserved for production decisions later.
  • Audio Track B (VT-3): VT-3 stereo → MOTU inputs 3/4 → Ableton Audio Track. The VT-3 character captured as printed audio.

Record both simultaneously. Blend or choose in the mix based on creative needs.

Live Performance Tips

  • Add a footswitch (optional 1/4-inch TRS) to the VT-3's footswitch input for hands-free bypass and preset recall while performing.
  • Assign the Launchkey 37's Custom Mode pads to MIDI Program Change 1–6 to recall VT-3 presets from a keyboard pad without reaching for the unit.
  • For vocoder with musical pitch: Launchkey 37 MIDI Out → VT-3 USB MIDI In. Play chords or melodies on the Launchkey while speaking into the SM58 for melodic vocoder phrases.
  • All downstream Ableton processing (EQ, compression, reverb) remains independent — route the MOTU input signal through the same vocal chain as the dry SM58 signal.

Built for fast lookups, cleaner routing decisions, and practical studio recall.
